title: Homepage rendered at 776–1779 px wide on every mobile viewport before fix
content: Validation against worker/src/homepage.ts served via `wrangler dev` at six viewports (320, 360, 375, 414, 768, 1280) showed `documentElement.scrollWidth` of 1779 at every viewport ≤ 414 and 1790 at 768 — i.e. the page was 5.5× viewport width on a 320 px screen and 4.7× on a 375 px screen. Visual evidence: content (hero, prose, doors, telemetry, three-points) all sat in the leftmost ~400 px of an effective 776–1779 px-wide canvas while the right portion was empty wasteland that required horizontal scroll to traverse. Root cause: every mobile-collapsing grid in the CSS used bare `1fr` (= `minmax(auto, 1fr)`), and `auto` resolves to the cell's intrinsic min-content width — which equals the longest unwrappable line in the `<pre class="code">` blocks the cells contained. The desktop-mode `.protocol` grid did the right thing with `repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr))`; mobile-mode just dropped the `minmax(0, …)` constraint when collapsing to one column. Affected grids: .hero-grid, .doors, .theatre-stage, .protocol, .telem-grid, .three-points, .foot-inner, plus an inline `style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr"` on the wire-inset wrapper at line 975.

title: Wire-inset shell snippets joined into 200-character single lines by template literal line continuation
content: Lines 979–997 of worker/src/homepage.ts — the four-line `curl` and `wscat` examples in the "Try the wire yourself" inset — used single `\` as the trailing shell line continuation. Because the entire HTML lives inside a TypeScript backtick template literal, `\` followed by a newline is the JavaScript line-continuation escape and is consumed (both `\` and the newline are removed from the resulting string). Net effect: the four-line `wscat -c "wss://..." \ -H "Auth..." \ -H "X-AMS-Stream-Name: sender" \ -x '{...}' -w 2` became one 211-character line in the rendered DOM, contributing ~700 px of additional minimum width to the wire-inset `<pre>` blocks. Other `<pre class="code">` blocks elsewhere (lines 1058, 1059, 1075, 1076) correctly use `\\` so the literal `\` survives template-literal evaluation; the wire-inset was the only block that used single backslashes.

title: Use minmax(0, 1fr) on every grid that collapses on mobile
content: All seven mobile-collapsing grids in worker/src/homepage.ts now use `grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr)` (or `minmax(0, 1fr) minmax(0, 1fr)` for the footer's two-column mobile layout) instead of bare `1fr`. This forces the grid cell's min-width to 0, allowing children with intrinsic min-content larger than the viewport (e.g. `<pre class="code">` with `white-space: pre`) to scroll horizontally inside their cells via their existing `overflow-x: auto` rather than expanding the cell — and therefore the page — to fit. Decision is local to homepage.ts; no impact on the worker, the wire, or any canon constraint. Reversible with low cost. The same pattern is the documented fix in CSS specs and Web Platform Tests for "1fr does not constrain min-content overflow"; this is just applying the well-known recipe.

title: Flex inputs in this repo must carry min-width: 0 on mobile
content: Two flex-row layouts on the homepage (`.field-row` for door inputs and `.agent-input` for theatre per-agent inputs) put a `<input flex:1>` next to a fixed-width `<button>`. The flex spec sets each child's `min-width: auto`, which for form controls resolves to a UA-defined intrinsic width that may exceed the available row space on 320 px screens — pushing the button outside the parent card by ~24 px. Both rows now carry `min-width: 0` on the row and the input under the mobile media query. Future flex layouts on this homepage that put an input next to a fixed-size sibling SHOULD apply the same pattern. This is documentation, not a wire/protocol/architecture constraint.

title: Static byte-extraction of TS template literals can synthesize bugs the runtime does not have
content: First diagnostic pass on this homepage extracted HOMEPAGE_HTML by slicing source bytes between the backticks, without evaluating the template literal. Loaded that file into a headless browser and observed `Unexpected token '^'` from a regex literal (`u.pathname.match(/^\\/([^/]+)\\/conversations\\/([^/]+)\\/?$/)` at line 1544). Reported this as a page-breaking JS bug. It was not. The actually-served HTML — produced by V8 evaluating the template literal — contains `\/` (one backslash, valid escaped slash in a regex), not `\\/` (two backslashes, which terminate the regex prematurely). Verified zero `pageerror` events when the homepage is served via `wrangler dev` and rendered via Playwright. The lesson generalizes: any verification of HTML+JS-in-template-literals must run through the actual worker (or a faithful template-literal evaluator) before claims are made; static byte-slicing produces a strict superset of real-world bugs. Cost of this lesson: one wasted "fix" attempt that introduced a real bug, immediately caught by re-verification, reverted, no harm shipped. Anchor: Axiom 4 — You Cannot Verify What You Did Not Observe (the served HTML, not its source).

title: Zero overflow at 320, 360, 375, 414, 768, 1280 verified against running worker
content: After the fix, capture-real.js drove a Playwright Chromium against `http://127.0.0.1:8787` (wrangler dev local mode) at six viewports. Result for every viewport: `documentElement.scrollWidth === window.innerWidth` (overflow = 0), `body.scrollWidth === window.innerWidth`, zero `pageerror` events, zero `console.error` events. Worker log confirms 200 OK on every `GET /` and 200 OK on `GET /healthz`. The six-viewport JSON report is preserved as part of the PR description. Companion screenshots: real-{320,360,375,414,768,1280}-{full,fold}.png and a 375-viewport before/after comparison comparison-375.png. Validation per the AGENTS.md fresh-session requirement is NOT satisfied by these in-session captures — see the H artifact below.

title: PR open at fix/mobile-responsiveness; needs fresh-session validation before merge
content: Branch fix/mobile-responsiveness contains one commit on top of main: 67 insertions / 16 deletions in worker/src/homepage.ts. Per AGENTS.md mode discipline ("validation requires a context break"), this turn produced execution evidence but cannot be its own validator — same session, same accumulated state, lenses-used-to-create are the lenses-used-to-evaluate. A fresh validator session SHOULD: (1) clone fix/mobile-responsiveness, (2) `cd worker && npm i && npx wrangler dev --local`, (3) load `http://127.0.0.1:8787/` in a real mobile browser or Playwright at 320/375/414, (4) confirm `documentElement.scrollWidth === window.innerWidth`, (5) tap-test the door and theatre inputs to confirm tap targets feel ≥44pt and iOS does not zoom on focus, (6) confirm wire-inset shell snippets render as four lines each (not joined). Then merge. The wire/protocol/architecture is untouched; this is purely a presentation-layer fix and does not require re-running SPEC §3.1 smoke.
